channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-20014] tgt_clean_by_tag()) ASSERTION( (!(tgt_is_increasing_xid_client(exp)) || (trd->trd_reply.lrd_xid <= xid)) ) failed
item.description:
req_can_reconstruct() can return -EPROTO which should be accounted in all related calls. Right now there are two places where discrepancies are possible:

  1. in tgt_handle_recovery() it is checked for 1 only, assuming 0 otherwise which is not true
  2. in tgt_check_resent() it is checked for not 0 case assuming it is 1 which is also not true

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
The problem I am trying to fix is the following assertion:

[3136811.974191] LustreError: 416861:0:(tgt_lastrcvd.c:1217:tgt_clean_by_tag()) ASSERTION( (!(tgt_is_increasing_xid_client(exp)) || (trd->trd_reply.lrd_xid <= xid)) ) failed: 
[3136811.974195] LustreError: 416861:0:(tgt_lastrcvd.c:1217:tgt_clean_by_tag()) LBUG
[3136811.975796] Kernel panic - not syncing: LBUG
[3136811.975802] Call Trace:
[3136811.975804]  dump_stack+0x41/0x60
[3136811.975808]  panic+0xe7/0x2ac
[3136811.975814]  lbug_with_loc.cold.8+0x1a/0x43 [libcfs]
[3136811.975830]  tgt_mk_reply_data+0x70f/0xb60 [ptlrpc]
[3136811.975956]  tgt_txn_stop_cb+0xacf/0xe10 [ptlrpc]
[3136811.976078]  dt_txn_hook_stop+0x77/0x110 [obdclass]
[3136811.976130]  osd_trans_stop+0x30f/0xb20 [osd_ldiskfs]
[3136811.976151]  ? jbd2__journal_start+0x8f/0x1f0 [jbd2]
[3136811.976156]  top_trans_stop+0x9f/0x10d0 [ptlrpc]
[3136811.976232]  ? _cond_resched+0x15/0x30
[3136811.976235]  lod_trans_stop+0x9b/0x340 [lod]
[3136811.976255]  mdd_trans_stop+0x29/0x172 [mdd]
[3136811.976273]  mdd_attr_set+0x5bd/0xd50 [mdd]
[3136811.976288]  ? mdt_reint_setattr+0xf14/0x1610 [mdt]
[3136811.976317]  mdt_reint_setattr+0xf14/0x1610 [mdt]
[3136811.976340]  ? ucred_set_audit_enabled.isra.18+0x1d/0x50 [mdt]
[3136811.976362]  ? old_init_ucred_common+0x267/0x5b0 [mdt]
[3136811.976384]  mdt_reint_rec+0x127/0x260 [mdt]
[3136811.976406]  mdt_reint_internal+0x4ac/0x7a0 [mdt]
[3136811.976427]  mdt_reint+0x5e/0x100 [mdt]
[3136811.976448]  tgt_request_handle+0xc0a/0x1b50 [ptlrpc]
[3136811.976526]  ? ptlrpc_nrs_req_get_nolock0+0xff/0x1f0 [ptlrpc]
[3136811.976596]  ? ptlrpc_update_export_timer+0x3d/0x520 [ptlrpc]
[3136811.976662]  ptlrpc_server_handle_request+0x25a/0xca0 [ptlrpc]
[3136811.976724]  ? lprocfs_counter_add+0x10e/0x180 [obdclass]
[3136811.976763]  ptlrpc_main+0xb45/0x13a0 [ptlrpc]
[3136811.976825]  ? ptlrpc_register_service+0xf30/0xf30 [ptlrpc]
[3136811.976885]  kthread+0x134/0x150
[3136811.976888]  ? set_kthread_struct+0x50/0x50
[3136811.976891]  ret_from_fork+0x1f/0x40

coredump shows the following:

rq_xid =        1850859457110848
exp_last_xid =  1850859621349375
lrd_xid =       1850859621948480

with very old deadline:
rq_deadline           = 1768207366,
exp_last_request_time = 1768207813,

and tag pointing to freed slot:
pb_tag = 1,
exp_used_slots = 0xff4d7a71be669e40,

request is just ordinary request with long passed deadline, and with tag for freed reply slot. That looks like while it was stuck in the middle of processing for some reason, there was RESENT request which was processed first, clearing reply slot and bumping lrd_xid. Exact scenario is still unclear though.

"Mikhail Pershin <mpershin@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/64648
Subject: LU-20014 mdt: check req_can_reconstruct() return code
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: ed9ab2d52a76c676c290b2921ac43a2b1febe343
